type: SURVEY

note: Adding new methods: ImageBlock.resolve_image_with_size(), AudioBlock.resolve_audio_with_size(), DocumentBlock.resolve_document_with_size() - each returns (BytesIO, int) tuple. Original resolve_image/resolve_audio/resolve_document methods unchanged. Will need to import Tuple from typing.

type: AGREE
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
note: Confirmed: no overlapping constructs. You add new resolve_*_with_size() methods (separate, new code); I modify the existing resolve_image/resolve_audio/resolve_document method bodies in place (adding zero-byte check before return). Since your new methods are separate additions and don't touch the existing method bodies, no merged code needed. I'll place my edits directly inside the existing method bodies; suggest you place your new methods immediately after each corresponding existing method to avoid line-range collisions.

type: DRAFT
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
region: resolve_image()/resolve_audio()/resolve_document() methods
note: Since you're modifying the bodies of resolve_image/resolve_audio/resolve_document (adding zero-byte validation), and I'm adding new sibling methods resolve_image_with_size/resolve_audio_with_size/resolve_document_with_size right after each, please share the exact final code of your three modified methods so I can place my additions cleanly after them without conflicting. My with_size methods will simply call the existing resolve_* method and then use buffer.seek(0,2)/tell()/seek(0) to get size - they won't duplicate your validation logic, so no logic overlap, just need exact text/line placement to avoid clashing hunks.

type: DRAFT
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
region: resolve_image() method
code: def resolve_image(self, as_base64: bool = False) -> BytesIO:
        """
        Resolve an image such that PIL can read it.

        Args:
            as_base64 (bool): whether the resolved image should be returned as base64-encoded bytes

        """
        buffer = resolve_binary(
            raw_bytes=self.image,
            path=self.path,
            url=str(self.url) if self.url else None,
            as_base64=as_base64,
        )
        buffer.seek(0, 2)
        size = buffer.tell()
        buffer.seek(0)
        if size == 0:
            raise ValueError("resolve_image returned zero bytes")
        return buffer
note: This is the exact final body of resolve_image. resolve_audio and resolve_document follow the identical pattern (see next messages). Place your resolve_image_with_size() method immediately after this one, ending with 'return buffer'.

code: def resolve_audio(self, as_base64: bool = False) -> BytesIO:
        """
        Resolve an audio such that PIL can read it.

        Args:
            as_base64 (bool): whether the resolved audio should be returned as base64-encoded bytes

        """
        buffer = resolve_binary(
            raw_bytes=self.audio,
            path=self.path,
            url=str(self.url) if self.url else None,
            as_base64=as_base64,
        )
        buffer.seek(0, 2)
        size = buffer.tell()
        buffer.seek(0)
        if size == 0:
            raise ValueError("resolve_audio returned zero bytes")
        return buffer
note: Place your resolve_audio_with_size() method immediately after this one.

type: DRAFT
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
region: resolve_document() method
code: def resolve_document(self) -> BytesIO:
        """
        Resolve a document such that it is represented by a BufferIO object.
        """
        buffer = resolve_binary(
            raw_bytes=self.data,
            path=self.path,
            url=str(self.url) if self.url else None,
            as_base64=False,
        )
        buffer.seek(0, 2)
        size = buffer.tell()
        buffer.seek(0)
        if size == 0:
            raise ValueError("resolve_document returned zero bytes")
        return buffer
note: Place your resolve_document_with_size() method immediately after this one. This completes all three draft methods for this feature.
